title: 2026-04-20 session context loss
situation: On 2026-04-20 the operator and Claude Code produced a mission scope reframe, a delivery plan, a seven-PR merge wave, and first scouting for benchmark tracks A and B. The fresh-agent recovery scenario starts after local Claude Code state is wiped, corrupted, or lost through compaction.
cold_start_prompt: I'm back. What were we working on, and what's the next step?
work_instruction_test: Identify the next concrete unblock for the recovery benchmark work, and state why a real pilot must not run yet.

baselines.name: no_memory
baselines.preserved_state: Nothing except the repository checkout and the cold-start prompt.

baselines.name: preserved_markdown_directory
baselines.preserved_state: The operator's Claude auto-memory markdown directory survives.

baselines.name: curated_handoff_doc
baselines.preserved_state: An operator-written STATUS-style handoff document for the scenario.

baselines.name: mimir_mode_1
baselines.preserved_state: Canonical Mimir log and Mimir access, with no other preserved local agent state.

ground_truth.text: Operator is Alain Dormehl, owner of Mimir and BES Studios.
Engagement protocol is Propose, Wait, Execute, Report, Stop.
CI quota is a hard rule: verify locally before every push because GitHub Actions minutes are limited.
Mimir stays private until the operator is happy it delivers on its promises; phase ordering is not a public deadline.
Fedora 43 tooling had a known ld.bfd issue on large Rust workspaces, with mold used as the practical linker path.
Main was at commit 6e6bcd6 after the 2026-04-20 merge wave.
The workspace had 463 tests passing at that point in the historical scenario.
GitHub Actions quota was exhausted as of 2026-04-20; recent CI failures in about two seconds with empty steps arrays were the quota fingerprint, not a workflow config bug.
Three planning docs were on main: roadmap to prime time, mission scope and recovery benchmark, and delivery plan.
The delivery plan had four pre-flip deliverables: parse-rate benchmark execution, recovery benchmark v0, Mode 1 client integration, and public-readiness pass.
Mimir's primary value proposition is catastrophic-loss recovery; token savings, cross-session continuity, and proactive recall are bonuses.
The three-pillar mission is BC/DR, memory graduation, and sanitized cross-agent sharing; Mimir is not a daily-memory replacement.

Agent-native at the runtime surface is load-bearing: MCP responses, retrieved payloads, and recovery digests should be token-dense and structured, while human-readable output belongs to observability surfaces.
The Phase 5 public flip is gated on deliverables A, B, C, and D completing, not on an external deadline.

The two librarian modes under consideration were extensions of the shipping design: Mode 1 as Claude-as-librarian plus client integration, and Mode 2 as a local Ollama arbiter implementing the already-specified InferenceProposer hook.
Two CHANGELOG conflicts were resolved during the 2026-04-20 merge wave by concatenating both Added sections without dropping content.
Four memory files were saved during the session and one was updated with the CI quota diagnostic fingerprint.
The immediate next step in the historical delivery plan was to scout A and B in parallel while CI quota was out; execution was blocked on a batched operator unblock.
Benchmark A's scaffold was verified ready: the corpus parsed 100/100 via verify_corpus.py on 2026-04-20.
There is no production Anthropic API dependency in Mimir; the Phase 3.2 benchmark used the API only as a measurement instrument.

staleness_tests.superseded_fact: The benchmark would use the Anthropic API as Mimir's operating surface.
staleness_tests.current_fact: Production has no API dependency; API use is measurement-only.
staleness_tests.expected_behavior: Do not surface the API-operating-surface framing as current.

staleness_tests.superseded_fact: The benchmark would compare five invented scenarios.
staleness_tests.current_fact: The accepted pattern is one worked example plus operator-provided real scenarios and ground truth.
staleness_tests.expected_behavior: Do not surface the five-invented-scenarios plan as current.

staleness_tests.superseded_fact: The initial merge plan was ambiguous: start with PR #1 and #6 myself, or ask the operator to review/merge.
staleness_tests.current_fact: The ambiguity was resolved by the operator's approval to proceed.
staleness_tests.expected_behavior: Do not surface the merge-plan ambiguity as unresolved.
